programme.title: Black '47
programme.desc: In 1847 an Irish mercenary fighting for the British army abandons his post to seek revenge on those responsible for the death of his family.

programme.title: Fall Down Dead
programme.desc: A serial killer stalks seven strangers in an office building during a blackout.

programme.title: Day of the Mummy
programme.desc: Jack Wells arrives in Egypt to search for a legendary diamond, and his search leads him to the tomb of King Neferu. When Jack disturbs his centuries-old state, the mad pharaoh rises from the dead with a bloodlust that cannot be quenched.

programme.title: Radioflash
programme.desc: In a dystopian future, a tech-savvy teenager and her father flee the city and seek refuge with her grandfather deep in the mountains of the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: VFW
programme.desc: A typical night for veterans at a VFW turns into a battle for survival when a desperate teen runs into the bar. When a gang of punks come looking for her, the vets use every weapon at their disposal to protect themselves from an unrelenting attack.

programme.title: Rogue Agent
programme.desc: Conman Robert Freegard poses as an undercover MI5 agent and kidnaps countless victims amidst a high-stakes manhunt, until the woman who fell for him brings him to justice.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: While making an instructional video, hunters become the prey after they trespass on government land.

programme.title: The Colony
programme.desc: Forced to live underground, survivors of an ice age embark on a mission to reach another outpost.

programme.title: ATM
programme.desc: A murderous assailant traps three people (Alice Eve, Josh Peck, Brian Geraghty) in a small kiosk containing an automatic teller machine.

programme.title: The Tax Collector
programme.desc: Two enforcers for a crime lord face an uncertain future when an old rival reappears.

programme.title: The Advent Calendar
programme.desc: An ex-dancer is given a mysterious advent calendar.

programme.title: Brawl in Cell Block 99
programme.desc: A desperate former boxer takes work as a drug courier and winds up in a jail where his enemies force him to commit acts of violence.

programme.title: Prisoners of the Ghostland
programme.desc: In Samurai Town, a ruthless bank robber gets sprung from jail by a warlord whose adopted granddaughter has gone missing. Strapped into a leather suit that will self-destruct in five days, the bandit sets off on a journey to find the young woman.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.desc: A same-sex couple moves to a small town with their 16-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Sorry!

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: The French Revolution sent shockwaves through Britain. While some watched transfixed, others were horrified. Simon Schama explores why the British proved immune to the siren call of liberty, equality and fraternity.

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Norma Jeane attends an audition at 20th Century Fox, where the head of casting suggests she change her name to Marilyn Monroe. After connecting with Joseph Schenck, Marilyn is able to get a job at Columbia Pictures, where she receives her first starring role. Upon renewal of her contract, the head of the studio makes an unwelcome advance which leads to Marilyn’s dismissal.

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Marilyn’s rise is nearly ruined when news breaks that she posed nude for a calendar earlier in her career. She uses this to her advantage to showcase her body confidence. Marilyn demonstrates her unique talent for comedy in Gentlemen Prefer Blondes and reaches superstar status whilst using her position to push for pay parity.

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Marilyn's forging a new identity for herself in NY, but when news breaks that she plans to create her own production company, Darryl Zanuck refuses to let her out of her contract.

programme.title: Reframed: Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Marilyn shines in her most celebrated comedy yet, Some Like It Hot. In the weeks before her death, Marilyn is on the cusp of a new and exciting future, making front pages with her skinny dip portraits and securing a triumphant new deal with 20th Century Fox.

programme.title: Being Stanley Baxter
programme.desc: TV and panto star Stanley Baxter had big success but an even bigger secret. With access to close family and friends, personal archive and recordings, we reveal his true story.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Semi-final of the quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take players so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. It is all about making connections between things which may appear, at first glance, not to be connected at all.

programme.title: India: Nature's Wonderland
programme.desc: The hidden wonders of India's spectacular natural world are revealed by wildlife expert Liz Bonnin, actress Freida Pinto and mountaineer Jon Gupta. Experience a village of birds, masks that come alive, the world's greatest mountain range and baby turtles erupting out of the sand. This is truly a land like no other.

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerksi explores the extraordinary science of heat. She reveals how heat is the hidden energy contained within matter, with the power to transform it from one state to another. Our ability to harness this fundamental law of science has led to some of humanity's greatest achievements, from the molten metals that enabled us to make tools, to the great engines of the Industrial Revolution powered by steam, to the searing heat of plasmas that offer almost unlimited power.

programme.title: Murder on the Victorian Railway
programme.desc: London 1864. On a Hackney bound train, a guard discovers blood in a first-class carriage - and a body on the railway embankment. For the first time, a murder has taken place on Britain's railways. Over a hundred years later, this single documentary for BBC Two uses the first-hand testimony of the people involved at the time to explore this unique event and how it provoked a huge public outcry and debate. The story quickly became a news sensation as the dangers of the brave new world of the train were laid bare. Over a hundred years later, the witnesses to this story may be long dead but their words survive in court transcripts, memoirs, letters and vivid journalism. These testimonies are now used to tell the story, taking the documentary deep into the 19th century to meet an extraordinary cross section of real-life Victorian London - from the engine driver who found the body to the detective in charge of the investigation. Who killed Mr Briggs?

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.desc: Stephen finds an enemy and then a friend when he tries to help the appropriately named Professor Furie overcome his tiredness.

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.desc: The medical staff must write academic research papers. Bob seizes the opportunity to trial a tranquiliser that might kick off his career as a private consultant.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: What happens when a literary novelist tries to write popular romantic fiction? To mark 100 years of romance publishers Mills and Boon, literary novelist Stella Duffy takes on the challenge of writing for them. Romantic fiction is a global phenomenon, and Mills and Boon are among the biggest names in the business. The company welcomes submissions from new authors, but as Duffy soon finds out, writing a Mills and Boon is harder than it looks. Help is at hand from the publishers themselves, a prolific Mills and Boon author and some avid romance fans, as Duffy's quest to create the perfect romantic novel takes her from London to Italy on a journey that is both an insight into the art of romantic fiction and the joy and frustration of writing itself.

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerksi explores the extraordinary science of heat. She reveals how heat is the hidden energy contained within matter, with the power to transform it from one state to another. Our ability to harness this fundamental law of science has led to some of humanity's greatest achievements, from the molten metals that enabled us to make tools, to the great engines of the Industrial Revolution powered by steam, to the searing heat of plasmas that offer almost unlimited power.

For the first time, a murder has taken place on Britain's railways. Over a hundred years later, this single documentary for BBC Two uses the first-hand testimony of the people involved at the time to explore this unique event and how it provoked a huge public outcry and debate. The story quickly became a news sensation as the dangers of the brave new world of the train were laid bare. Over a hundred years later, the witnesses to this story may be long dead but their words survive in court transcripts, memoirs, letters and vivid journalism. These testimonies are now used to tell the story, taking the documentary deep into the 19th century to meet an extraordinary cross section of real-life Victorian London - from the engine driver who found the body to the detective in charge of the investigation. programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Two more of round one’s highest-scoring losing teams have a second chance to qualify for the second round. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take players so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. A team of three lads from the Cambridge Quiz Society pit their wits against a trio of Oxford Librarians with specialisms as diverse as Comparative Slavonic Linguistics, Classics and Theology. They compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random, from Goldeneye to The Kilns to Hill Top to Haworth Parsonage.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Marilyn Monroe was, and perhaps will always be, Hollywood’s biggest female film icon and the sexiest of cinema's sex symbols, lighting up the screen in several cinema classics – including one of cinema’s greatest comedies, Some Like It Hot. The aura surrounding her has been enhanced by a sense of mystery that comes from the fact she was very rarely interviewed. But anyone who knew or worked with her was guaranteed to be asked about their personal 'Marilyn moments'. Here we tell Marilyn's story through the insights and anecdotes of friends, directors and fellow actors who spoke about her over the years on a selection of BBC programmes. With archive interviews featuring stars like Jane Russell, Jack Lemmon and Tony Curtis, as well as directors like Billy Wilder and John Huston, and Marilyn's former husband Arthur Miller, these conversations paint a picture of a star who conquered Hollywood despite serious battles with her inner demons, and reveal what it was like for those who got to enter into the orbit of one of cinema's greatest stars.

programme.title: Some Like It Hot
programme.desc: 1929. Musicians Joe and Jerry accidentally witness the St Valentine's Day massacre. The boys decide to make a getaway by joining a jazz band heading for Florida. There's just one problem - it's an all-girl group. A comic masterpiece from director Wilder, with memorable performances.

programme.title: Nobody's Perfect: The Making of Some Like It Hot
programme.desc: Revealing documentary about the creation and shooting of the acclaimed comedy movie Some Like It Hot. Stars Jack Lemmon and Tony Curtis - and other members of the cast and crew, including producer Walter Mirisch - remember the making of writer-director Billy Wilder's classic film. Using extraordinary behind-the-scenes colour footage and candid on-set photographs taken by stills photographer Richard Miller (also interviewed), the documentary provides a surprisingly intimate and revealing but nevertheless hilarious portrait of the making of one of Hollywood's best-loved and greatest films.

programme.title: My Week with Marilyn
programme.desc: It is 1956, and The Prince and the Showgirl is being made at Pinewood Studios in London, directed by and starring Laurence Olivier, who has recruited one of the biggest Hollywood stars of all time, Marilyn Monroe, to co-star with him. Colin Clark, a junior assistant on the production, is assigned the job of looking after Marilyn and keeps a journal about the week he spent with her. It proves to be a turbulent experience as Marilyn is going through marital problems with her husband and uses Colin as a much-needed sounding board for all her pent-up neurosis. Acclaimed drama from BBC Films based on Clark's real-life diaries.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: AI Decoded examines a dramatic week for Elon Musk that could reshape the future of artificial intelligence. A US court has rejected Musk's claims against OpenAI, bringing a long-running feud with co-founder Sam Altman into sharp focus and offering a rare glimpse inside one of the world's most influential AI companies. At the same time, SpaceX is preparing for what could be the largest stock market listing in history - a move that may value the company at up to $1.75 trillion and make Musk the world's first human trillionaire.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Shiona McCallum meets the medical expert testing out the future of prosthetics following his own near-fatal cycling accident. Paul Carter heads inside Amazon's AI chip lab in Austin, Texas. Zoe Kleinman visits the UK Dementia Research Institute to see how AI is being used to improve diagnosis and help find treatments. programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Morning Live is BBC One’s flagship daytime programme, bringing you the best advice from familiar faces across the BBC, original journalism and investigations, and a sparkle of Strictly magic

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: The team hear the incredible story of one Edinburgh woman left devastated when an online romance turned out to be a scam – with a sophisticated AI trick luring her into parting with thousands of pounds. We also go behind the scenes with the banking team charged with breaking the scammers’ spell in order to stop their unsuspecting customers from handing over their life savings. And Scam Interceptors’ Nick Stapleton reveals how so-called hustle kingdoms power a sinister global industry of romance scams.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two dog-loving developers tackle epic projects in Wales and Norfolk. Also, a seasoned developer is looking to move quickly and economically in Nottingham.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of carers compete to make the most profit, as twin sisters take on a mum-and-daughter duo at a bustling antiques fair in Newark.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie speaks to a friend about caring for a child with cancer before joining community nursing in action - and faces her own fear, needles!

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson is joined by his uncle Matheson as the pair head to the Scottish Borders for a weekend in the outdoors to relax and reset. On their way to their favourite fishing spot in Coldingham, they have a go at hedge laying, before Matheson relives his youth riding horses on the beach.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a young couple search for their first home together in the Oxfordshire countryside. They are hoping for space to accommodate a rabbit palace and an impressive collection of houseplants. Alistair also pays a visit to the University of Oxford Botanic Garden, where he discovers how nature is inspiring exciting new technological developments.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo and Joe are in Stockport to help Sasha and Rick transform their long, muddy, unloved garden into a wild English wonderland. With unsafe concrete steps making the patio hard to access, it’s no place for daughter Jocelyn or the new baby on the way, and hosting friends and family is a real challenge. The couple dream of a vibrant, wildlife-friendly space, bursting with colourful flowers and plants, where their children can grow up with adventure and magic all around. They want a practical design too, with a clear divide between patio and grass so that their rescue dog Nelly can roam without tracking mud indoors. Having spent the last 18 months renovating their home while raising Jocelyn and preparing for their second child, Sasha and Rick are ready for their garden to finally match their family’s energy and joy. Can the team deliver their dream sanctuary on a £4,000 budget?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four take on the Bridge of Lies. In this episode, it's family and friends Lynne, Derek, John and Kenny. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money, step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns, but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Rose Matafeo. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant steps up to protect his family, Zack purposely raises suspicions, and Eddie and Harry clash.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: It’s the quarter final, and Alan and the designers head to the UK’s 'town of books', Hay-on-Wye, in Wales, to transform two high-street shops. One is a specialist guitar shop and the other a gift shop featuring local arts and crafts. Working in pairs, they need to improve layout, footfall and visual merchandising before facing critiques from Michelle and retail expert Ross Bailey.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Zoe Ball follows in her ancestors’ footsteps in a journey that takes her from Scotland to Cornwall as she uncovers stories of resilience and the importance of family over place.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 2010. Niall and Ava face major life changes as Ruben struggles with his relationship. An impulsive choice between Mona and Niall has serious consequences for all involved.

programme.title: The Photograph
programme.desc: Love stories set in the past and the present intertwine when the finding of a photograph leads an estranged daughter on a journey of discovery into her late mother’s early life.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Morning Live is BBC One’s flagship daytime programme, bringing you the best advice from familiar faces across the BBC, original journalism and investigations, and a sparkle of Strictly magic

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: When a viewer contacts Rip Off Britain after suspecting the online task work he’s been completing might not be for real, the team investigate the sophisticated world of so-called task scams. Plus justice for the victims of one dodgy spray foam insulation firm. Seven years after Rip Off Britain went undercover as a sales operative, the team hear how that footage formed part of the investigation that put the man responsible behind bars.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is overcome by the amount of beige on show in a Telford flat. Dion heads downwards to reveal an unexpected bonus in Leeds. In Suffolk, there are character features for Jacqui to uncover.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A well-groomed Charlie Ross pays a visit to a barbershop museum in Norfolk to see a huge collection of antique shaving mugs. One scuttle-shaped mug was patented in around 1867. In total, proud museum owner Richard Finney has 500 on display. Aiming to shave off a few pounds on their purchases are two teams of families shopping at a massive antiques centre in King’s Lynn. In red are father and daughter Simon and Ellen, and in blue are father and son Dave and James. With maths teacher Ellen keeping tabs on the reds’ budget, scientist Simon hopes their chemistry will generate profits. Meanwhile, the blues have a close encounter with a model of E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial, but at £245, he’s not coming home with them. Wielding his gavel in neighbouring Lincolnshire is auctioneer Colin Young. He shares his estimates with Charlie before the teams’ items go under the hammer. Profit or loss, one team comes out on top – but which one?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie’s juggling college and family life, learning about teenage mental health, online pressures and autism - and how care reaches far beyond the classroom.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: The Rev Richard Coles joins Robson to explore the Northumberland coastline. They take a Canadian canoe trip along the River Coquet, visit the hidden gem of Walkworth's secret hermitage, make the crossing to Lindisfarne for a photography class and have a go at rope work.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In Somerset, Micky from the busy seaside town of Hove is ready to fulfil a long-held dream of escaping to the countryside. She wants a property with lots of land for her big, beloved rescue dog, and Jules Hudson is there to answer her prayers with a line-up of standout properties that fit the bill. Jules also tries his hand at traditional screen printing when he visits a small studio specialising in designs inspired by nature.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and Flo head to Shrewsbury to help James and his sister Maria transform his plain, uninspired garden into a Doctor Who-inspired sci-fi dream. With little more than a fence, a small shed and a table and chairs, the space is ready for a complete regeneration! A lifelong Doctor Who and sci-fi fan, James envisions a low-maintenance, alien-themed haven complete with a TARDIS-style shed, futuristic planting, a circular pathway and a tranquil water feature. With Maria’s support and Charlie and Flo’s creativity, can this ordinary garden be turned into an otherworldly escape, all on a £5,000 budget? James, who has Asperger’s syndrome, has been living independently in his own home since the passing of their father in 2021. His sister Maria and her husband live just a mile away, and together they form a close-knit and supportive family. James’s lifelong passion for classic Doctor Who and all things sci-fi has inspired this ambitious project, a fun, thematic garden where he can relax, enjoy nature and truly feel at home in his very own alien world.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, family members Anna, Gemma, Zoe and Andreas – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ria Lina. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: J B Gill and Angellica Bell bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It’s the day of Vicki and Ross’s wedding, but will it go ahead? Priya gets a shock.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It’s semi-finals time, and the eight remaining cooks are ready to battle it out for a chance at victory. Anna and Grace ramp things up with their biggest challenges yet.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda finds out that a big house on her street is owned by an old lady who is planning to move out. So, she inserts herself into her life, hoping to get first refusal on the sale.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Ken forces Richard to accompany him to Aviemore so that he can help him navigate the QR code scanner at the train station ticket barrier. However, a mix-up on the platform turns what should be a simple day trip into an almighty fiasco.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: After solving the Andie Bell case, Pip is forced to confront the consequences of what her investigation exposed. It may have cleared Sal’s name, but it tore Cara’s family apart, and the guilt weighs heavily on Pip. In an attempt to rebuild her relationship with Cara, Pip releases a podcast to tell the world the real story. The only person yet to face justice is Max Hastings, and as his trial approaches, Pip is determined to help his victims find closure. However, the prosecution’s case begins to falter when Becca Bell withdraws from testifying. Unwilling to accept this, Pip musters the courage to visit Becca in prison with the hope of convincing her that giving evidence still matters. Pip wants to focus on supporting Ravi at the upcoming memorial for his brother, but when a mysterious note promising information on Max appears in her locker, Pip must make a difficult decision. Little Kilton comes together to say goodbye to both Andie Bell and Sal Singh. The end of one dark chapter gives way to another, as Cara finds herself in a dangerous situation, and Jamie Reynolds, Connor’s brother and a key witness in Max’s trial, disappears without warning.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip hits a dead end and rethinks. Digging into Jamie’s recent behaviour and last movements, she realises he may not be an innocent victim after all.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return in an episode packed with some of the best bits from series 19. A stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal amazing stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Among the guests joining Lee Mack and David Mitchell are Lucy Beaumont, Bob Mortimer, Jamelia, John Kearns, Harriet Kemsley, Yinka Bokinni, Gyles Brandreth, Dianne Buswell, Mark Chapman, Montell Douglas, Tasha Ghouri, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Will Kirk, David Morrissey, Josh Pugh and Holly Willoughby.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: The team bring the victim of an international investment scam network to meet one of the investigative journalists who uncovered it. At least 32,000 people lost more than £200m – could you be one of them? We have advice on what to do. And also on the programme, the Liverpool fan who was left empty-handed when he tried to buy tickets from an unofficial seller.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion’s in Sheffield, where he feels there is scope to double the size of a property. He is also in Nottingham with layout changes on the menu. Jacqui offers a 16th-century history lesson in Windsor.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Roo Irvine and the teams go antiques shopping in Northamptonshire with experts Gary Pe and Tim Weeks. Roo also brings along a mysterious antique to quiz the locals as to what they think it is.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie’s halfway through her college course and growing in confidence. On placement, she meets home carer Gary, who looks after his partner, Lisa.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson is joined by Invictus Games gold medal winner JJ Chalmers. The pair head to Killicrankie, where they zipwire across the River Garry, before heading to their digs for the night. The following day, the pair take a sledge ride through stunning countryside and then head to the Blackruthven Estate, where they are given a drystone walling lesson.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams helps a young family who have outgrown their London flat and now need a new home in rural Suffolk, with enough room for two growing children. The exciting property line-up includes a mystery house that has six centuries of stories to tell, some of which are written on its walls! Aside from ancient houses, Briony is excited to meet a fellow baking enthusiast who, after making his own escape to the county a year ago, now sells cakes and runs a supper club from his home.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Lee and Diarmuid are in Croydon to help sisters Lynette and Natalie rescue their vast, sun-baked plot, where weeds have taken over, veggie beds are impossible to reach and a polytunnel and pallets leave little room for peace. The sisters dream of a retreat garden where they can reconnect with nature – a hidden, tranquil space filled with scents, sounds and colour. They’d love raised, no-dig beds for veggies and propagation, plus a spot to sit and just relax. Having spent the last 18 months living together and supporting their community through wilderness gardening projects, Lynette and Natalie are ready to create a sanctuary of their own. Can the team transform this cluttered space into a peaceful retreat for £5,500?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, friends Louise, Emily, Laura and Beth – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Ria Lina. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Lauren Laverne bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos erupts at Vicki and Ross’s wedding, and Denise receives some news.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals continue, and just seven extraordinary home cooks remain in the competition. It’s time for them to dig even deeper into their culinary books of tricks.

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police concerns grow about the mental state of two missing teens - 16-year-old Larissa has disappeared after an argument with her mum, and 18-year-old Bobby may be a suicide risk.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate.

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Lighthearted discussion of the day's news.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents this high-stakes quiz, joined by a celebrity team featuring rugby player and podcaster Joe Marler, actor Jennie McAlpine, comedian Geoff Norcott and presenter Jean Johannson as they take on the Bridge of Lies in support of their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy’s recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommy’s next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mum out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair. It’s vital for Tommy’s mental health that he keeps physically active at all times, so he sets his sights on an ambitious new goal - a 100km triathlon. Meanwhile, John worries that the physical toll from this could jeopardise the chances of Tommy recovering and landing a big money fight. As Tommy throws himself into training for the triathlon, one question remains - can he avoid getting injured, or will this new challenge end his boxing career for good?

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since their separation last year, but John’s worries grow as Tommy takes on a 100km triathlon, pushing his body to new physical limits. Before the challenge, Tommy takes some downtime at the spa with his mates, preparing for a huge press push ahead of his series premiere. He’s come a long way since he and Molly separated last year, and the truth hits home when he sees his face on a billboard - and shares the moment with Molly. The premiere brings together family and friends, all celebrating how far Tommy has come. But there’s no time to rest - he heads to the south of France for the triathlon, where Molly and Bambi surprise him at the finishing line. Even with the triathlon behind him, Tommy’s pursuit of physical challenges continues as he gears up for the Great North Run the following week. Riding a high, he is suddenly hit with a backlash on social media, leaving him to wonder what he can do to catch a break.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy’s childhood friend Chris asks him to do a book reading at the school where they once studied. Tommy faces his nerves and steps up, inspiring students to dream big and believe anything is possible. Tragedy then strikes with the passing of boxing legend Ricky Hatton - a devastating loss for his friends, family and Manchester. Tommy trained with Ricky for 18 months, and his friend and mentor’s passing reminds him how important it is to live life to the fullest. Tommy sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible: climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. Battling brutal conditions and exhaustion, he pushes through to finish with only minutes to spare, greeted at the summit by an emotional John. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed. He reflects on where he was last autumn and everything he’s achieved over the past year, and looks forward to a brighter future.

programme.title: Hunting the Rolex Rippers
programme.desc: More and more luxury watches are being snatched off UK streets. In 2022, there was an estimated 60 per cent increase in watch thefts worldwide. It is now the crime of choice for many criminals, seen as easier and more lucrative than drug dealing. In this documentary, journalist Tir Dhondy gets access, for the first time ever, to the gang members behind the crime. She meets the teenage moped riders snatching watches off the street, as well as a gang leader who runs a team of teenage watch rippers and female watch spotters. Tir also comes face-to-face with the 16-year-old who used a machete to steal singer Aled Jones’s £17,000 Rolex in broad daylight.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Single, penniless poet Ruby has recently moved back home to live with her mum, Jen. When their strict Presbyterian neighbours descend on the home for her spoiled sister Eliza's engagement party, Ruby’s only job is to not shame the family...

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve is in search of one of the most spectacular events on earth - bait balls. These wildlife events are so epic that they can be seen from space - but they won’t be easy to find.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve searches for deadly animals living in the capital of Suriname, from snakes in people's gardens to snail-catching kites and one of the deadliest snakes on earth.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: After more than 11,000km across eight countries, the teams face one final test of endurance as they race to claim victory at the finish line in Hatgal, Mongolia.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Six months after the race, all five teams reunite to relive their 12,000km journey from Palermo to Mongolia, with unseen moments and unprecedented behind-the-scenes access

programme.title: Louis Theroux
programme.desc: Louis Theroux looks back at his earliest documentaries and explores how some people’s most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society.

programme.title: Louis Theroux
programme.desc: Louis explores how America has managed to take our darkest desires, commodify them and turn them into a saleable product. From his memorable Weird Weekends episodes on porn and rap to films about Las Vegas and the US opioid crisis, this episode explores a very human tendency to jump to the dark side when seeking out pleasure – whether through indulgence, risk-taking or the joy of doing something seen as ‘bad’ or 'wrong’. Louis re-connects with porn-star JJ, who over 20 years ago was just making his way in the adult industry but has since turned his back on that world in favour of a new life in eastern Europe. And Louis catches up once more with Katilia from Heroin Town, whom he last saw hopelessly addicted to drugs, with little sign of turning her life around.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: Twenty-year-old Alexander McCartney, already known to police, is arrested and his devices seized after a 12-year-old girl in Scotland reports a serious catfishing abuse incident online. McCartney gives a ‘no comment’ interview while in custody, and without enough physical evidence to charge him, he is released on bail. Back home at his parents’ house, McCartney’s bail conditions are supposed to prevent him from using devices that can connect to the internet. Meanwhile, a second report comes in to the police about another catfishing abuse victim linked to online activity from McCartney’s home address. This time, it involves a young girl in New Zealand. As the police continue to gather up enough physical evidence to charge McCartney, the Cyber Crime Centre completes the analysis of one of his devices, revealing a prolific online predator at a scale never before seen by police in Northern Ireland.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: With hundreds of thousands of images of child abuse discovered on McCartney’s devices, the race is on to get him remanded into custody. McCartney is rearrested in the early hours of the morning, at which time police discover McCartney’s phone on his bedside table, where his final victim was abused on Snapchat just hours before. The US Department of Homeland Security notify the police in Northern Ireland of two more catfishing victims, this time in the United States, and a multiagency international investigation is triggered. As McCartney is remanded into custody, police across the world work together to identify and safeguard the thousands of victims discovered on McCartney’s devices. Police are especially keen to identify and locate a 14-year-old girl in Oregon, whose images McCartney used to catfish hundreds of other children. With McCartney due in court to plead guilty to charges of making indecent of images of children and blackmail, the case takes a different turn when Homeland Security finds an obituary page for Cimarron Thomas, one of his victims in West Virginia.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: As police piece together the last few minutes of Cimarron’s life, they realise McCartney was still online threatening her when she tragically shot herself. Police and prosecutors move quickly to establish if there’s a case for manslaughter. Police from Northern Ireland travel to West Virginia to conduct the manslaughter investigation, where they learn that Cimarron’s own family had no idea why she took her own life. In a tragic turn, they learn that 18 months after her death, Cimarron’s father had also died by suicide. In order to charge McCartney with manslaughter, the police have to prove that he foresaw the risk to her life. As they comb through the cyber evidence from his devices, they build a case for manslaughter and charge him. The case sets new legal precedent in the UK. More than five years after the police investigation began, McCartney is finally convicted and sentenced.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Calamity James is a slapstick comedy about a young man, James, who is cursed with ‘acute misfortune syndrome’. James tries to reconnect with his dad after being thrown out by his mum for setting fire to their house at a family barbecue. In this world, chronic bad luck exists and the unbelievable is the everyday.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Life is hard, so Callum and Jordan, two emotionally unassembled young lads from Northern Ireland, start the Funboys’ Fun Club: a playgroup for childish young men to engage in wholesome mucking about. But when a juice party goes awry, Callum’s ultra-religious fiancée Morgan kicks them out, and the Funboys must search for a new HQ. When they come across Marty, a catatonically depressed 60-year-old, can they bring joy back to his life and prove that fun won't necessarily lead to an eternity spent in raging hellfire?

programme.title: Louis Theroux
programme.desc: Louis Theroux looks back at his earliest documentaries and explores how some people’s most fervent beliefs can bring them into conflict with mainstream society.

programme.title: Louis Theroux

Louis re-connects with porn-star JJ, who over 20 years ago was just making his way in the adult industry but has since turned his back on that world in favour of a new life in eastern Europe. And Louis catches up once more with Katilia from Heroin Town, whom he last saw hopelessly addicted to drugs, with little sign of turning her life around.

programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall is in Zambia to witness ten million fruit bats undertake the world’s largest aerial migration. However, these bats are a buffet for Africa’s top three eagles.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall is in Sydney, Australia, searching for the world’s most venomous spider, the fastest striking snake and an ocean dragon that is a camouflaged assassin.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: It’s the quarter final, and Alan and the designers head to the UK’s 'town of books', Hay-on-Wye, in Wales, to transform two high-street shops. One is a specialist guitar shop and the other a gift shop featuring local arts and crafts. Working in pairs, they need to improve layout, footfall and visual merchandising before facing critiques from Michelle and retail expert Ross Bailey.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: After solving the Andie Bell case, Pip is forced to confront the consequences of what her investigation exposed. It may have cleared Sal’s name, but it tore Cara’s family apart, and the guilt weighs heavily on Pip. In an attempt to rebuild her relationship with Cara, Pip releases a podcast to tell the world the real story. The only person yet to face justice is Max Hastings, and as his trial approaches, Pip is determined to help his victims find closure. However, the prosecution’s case begins to falter when Becca Bell withdraws from testifying. Unwilling to accept this, Pip musters the courage to visit Becca in prison with the hope of convincing her that giving evidence still matters. Pip wants to focus on supporting Ravi at the upcoming memorial for his brother, but when a mysterious note promising information on Max appears in her locker, Pip must make a difficult decision. Little Kilton comes together to say goodbye to both Andie Bell and Sal Singh. The end of one dark chapter gives way to another, as Cara finds herself in a dangerous situation, and Jamie Reynolds, Connor’s brother and a key witness in Max’s trial, disappears without warning.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Pip hits a dead end and rethinks. Digging into Jamie’s recent behaviour and last movements, she realises he may not be an innocent victim after all.

programme.title: Radio 1’s Big Weekend
programme.desc: Radio 1’s Big Weekend returns to Sunderland’s Herrington Country Park for the first time since 2005, and we’re bringing you the biggest moments from an unforgettable weekend of live music. Expect standout performances from some of the world’s biggest stars, including headliners Fatboy Slim, Zara Larsson and Olivia Dean, plus many more as we relive the action from across all three days on the Main Stage and the New Music Stage.

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Mobeen’s plan to get to Aqsa goes wrong, but before he can accept Uncle Shady’s drastic solution, a familiar face shows up with an offer of their own.

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Nida offers to get Mobeen a fake passport if he can steal something from the Turks. Will help from Uncle Shady and his big drill be enough to pull it off?

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Tensions run high as the gang debate what to do with the stolen money. Jerry reveals his true colours, Emre investigates the theft, and Uncle Shady offers Mobeen a lifeline.

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: On a plane alone, Mobeen recounts how it all started, with his teenage memories of the first job he did for Khan and the beginning of his brotherhood with Nate and Eight.

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: In Turkey, Istanbul offers many distractions, and Mobeen struggles to keep the gang focused on their mission to rescue Aqsa and kill Khan.

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: Mobeen and the gang face Khan in a final showdown. Can Mobeen save Aqsa and his friends to get back home to the ends?

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: On a Bradford estate, Lenny and Mullet find an abandoned car and turn it into a DIY spaceship. A kitchen-sink science fiction comedy about estate childhoods, big dreams and escaping without going anywhere.

It may have cleared Sal’s name, but it tore Cara’s family apart, and the guilt weighs heavily on Pip.
